title: O Lord, My God, Most Earnestly

verses.text: O Lord, my God, most earnestly my heart would seek thy face, within thy holy house once more to see thy glorious grace. Apart from thee I long and thirst, and nought can satisfy; I wander in a desert land where all the streams are dry.

The loving-kindness of my God is more than life to me; so I will bless thee while I live and lift my pray'r to thee. In thee my soul is satisfied, my darkness turns to light, and joyful meditations fill the watches of the night.

My Savior, 'neath thy shelt'ring wings my soul delights to dwell; still closer to thy side I press, for near thee all is well. My soul shall conqer ev'ry foe, upholden by thy hand; thy people shall rejoice in God, thy saints in glory stand.
